Batch-create multiple FMOD events in one script: set folder, route to bus via event.mixerInput.output, assign bank via event.relationships.banks.add(bank). Avoids multiple round-trips and TCP timeout risk. Use studio.project.model.Bank.findInstances() to find bank by name. Always save at end.

Complete LoopRegion creation for a looping event. A LoopRegion requires 4 things: (1) position property, (2) length property, (3) markerTrack relationship pointing to the event's first markerTrack, (4) timeline relationship pointing to the event's timeline. Missing either relationship causes isOneShot() to remain true. Always verify with ev.isOneShot() === false after creation.

Set event bus routing after event creation. New events default to the master bus which prevents bank builds. Always route to the correct game bus. For this project: weapon/skill events → "Skill" bus ({7da7a353-b064-4982-b453-d4873b91d499}). Find the correct bus by checking similar existing events: ev.mixerInput.output.id and ev.mixerInput.output.name.

When FMOD Studio scripting console is unresponsive or crashes, read the FMOD project XML files directly from disk to research object structures, relationships, and property values. Event XMLs at Metadata/Event/{GUID}.xml contain the full object graph including all child objects (Timeline, MarkerTrack, LoopRegion, MixerInput, etc.) with their properties and relationships. This is a reliable fallback for research when the live scripting API is unavailable.

Project convention: the _LP suffix on event names means the event is a looping sound. Every _LP event MUST have a LoopRegion object on its timeline. When creating a batch of events, check each name for the _LP suffix and add a LoopRegion automatically. The complete event creation checklist: (1) create event in correct folder, (2) add tracks and sounds, (3) if name ends with _LP → create LoopRegion, (4) set bus routing to correct game bus (not master), (5) save project.
Reduce amb_StartingForest_LP master volume by 3 dB (-7.5 → -10.5)
Set amb_StartingForest_LP masterTrack.mixerGroup.volume from -16.5 to -19.5 dB
Apply SnapshotCommandReverb template routing to event: Reverb return with ThreeEQ/Delay/Gain, Submix group with MixerSend, re-route existing tracks through Submix
Create event in a named folder by resolving folder via findInstances matching name and parent name
